nodes.context: Salvadoran criminal organization specializing in drug trafficking, active 1990s–2018.
Drug cartels from Colombia that manufactured drugs transported by Texis Cartel.
Drug cartels from Mexico that Texis Cartel worked with.
Drug trafficking group that received protection from Salvadoran officials alongside Texis Cartel.
Criminal street gang in El Salvador, hired by Texis Cartel for intimidation and killings.
Criminal street gang in El Salvador, hired by Texis Cartel for intimidation and killings.

edges.evidence_quote: The cartel transported drugs manufactured by Colombian drug cartels through northern El Salvador towards North America.
edges.context: Texis Cartel transported drugs produced by Colombian cartels.

edges.evidence_quote: It typically worked with drug cartels from Colombia and Mexico.
edges.context: Texis Cartel worked with Mexican drug cartels.

edges.evidence_quote: the United Nations confirmed that the Texis Cartel, along with the Los Perrones drug trafficking group, had received protection from Salvadoran government officials.
edges.context: Both groups received protection from Salvadoran officials, but no direct relationship between them is stated.

edges.evidence_quote: The cartel also hired gang members from MS-13 and 18th Street gang to intimidate and kill opponents if necessary.
edges.context: Texis Cartel hired MS-13 members for intimidation and killings.

edges.evidence_quote: The cartel also hired gang members from MS-13 and 18th Street gang to intimidate and kill opponents if necessary.
edges.context: Texis Cartel hired 18th Street gang members for intimidation and killings.
